forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KWJ3PSVZTXYZDX1ASZ3GN09M
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8b80
+title: 'queryAST op: runtime S-expression queries'
+---
+## What
+Create `Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/Ops/QueryAST.swift` — port of `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/query_ast.rs`. Compile a user-supplied tree-sitter S-expression query at runtime for a named language (via `Languages.all`), run it against files on disk under the root (filtered to that language's extensions, gitignore-aware), return `QueryASTResult { matches: [ASTMatch(file, captures)], filesScanned }` with a max-results cap. Invalid query text → thrown typed error with the tree-sitter message, not a crash.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A `(function_item name: (identifier) @name)` query over a rust fixture returns the expected capture names and ranges
+- [ ] Malformed query throws a descriptive error; unknown language throws
+- [ ] `maxResults` truncates and reports `filesScanned` accurately
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsCodeContextTests/QueryASTTests.swift`: capture correctness on fixtures, error paths, cap behavior
+- [ ] Run `swift test --filter QueryASTTests` → all pass
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

+Create `Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/Ops/QueryAST.swift` — port of `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/query_ast.rs`. Compile a user-supplied tree-sitter S-expression query at runtime for a named language (via `Languages.all`), run it against files on disk under the root — **enumerated via the shared `Walker` (gitignore-aware), filtered to that language's extensions; do not re-implement gitignore semantics** — and return `QueryASTResult { matches: [ASTMatch(file, captures)], filesScanned }` with a max-results cap. Invalid query text → thrown typed error with the tree-sitter message, not a crash.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] A `(function_item name: (identifier) @name)` query over a rust fixture returns the expected capture names and ranges
 - [ ] Malformed query throws a descriptive error; unknown language throws
-- [ ] `maxResults` truncates and reports `filesScanned` accurately
+- [ ] `maxResults` truncates and reports `filesScanned` accurately; gitignored files are never scanned

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsCodeContextTests/QueryASTTests.swift`: capture correctness on fixtures, error paths, cap behavior
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsCodeContextTests/QueryASTTests.swift`: capture correctness on fixtures, error paths, cap behavior, gitignore exclusion
 - [ ] Run `swift test --filter QueryASTTests` → all pass

 ## Workflow

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,4 +1,26 @@
 ---
+comments:
+- actor: wballard
+  id: 01kwk87p2a73sv6eecwhgabppy
+  text: |-
+    Implemented Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/Ops/QueryAST.swift per plan: compiles a user-supplied tree-sitter S-expression query at runtime for a language resolved from Languages.all (case-insensitive match), enumerates files via the existing Walker.enumerateFiles(rootDirectory:extensions:) (gitignore-aware, no reimplemented gitignore logic), parses/queries each file, and returns QueryASTResult{matches:[ASTMatch(file,captures:[ASTCapture])], filesScanned, truncated} capped by QueryASTOptions.maxResults (default 50).
+
+    Key design notes:
+    - Added CodeContextError.query(String) case for malformed-query and unknown-language failures (both throw, never crash).
+    - Widened Chunker.extractTextAndRange(of:in:) from private to internal and changed its return shape from (text, range) to (text, startByte, endByte) so QueryAST.makeCapture reuses the exact same UTF-16-NSRange -> UTF-8-byte-offset conversion Chunker already used, instead of duplicating the byte math. This matters because SwiftTreeSitter's Parser parses via UTF-16LE internally, so raw node.byteRange is NOT UTF-8 bytes -- must go through Range(node.range, in: source) + utf8.distance(from:to:).
+    - Files are sorted by path before scanning for deterministic match ordering (Walker's own enumeration order is unspecified).
+    - Renamed local Language values to `grammar` (not `language`) inside QueryAST.run to avoid shadowing the `language: String` parameter.
+
+    Followed TDD: wrote Tests/FoundationModelsCodeContextTests/QueryASTTests.swift first (11 tests), watched it fail to compile (QueryAST didn't exist), then implemented to green.
+
+    Went through 3 review cycles via mcp__sah__review (code-context review engine):
+    1. Doc comment formatting, initial extractTextAndRange visibility.
+    2. tsLanguage naming (renamed to grammar), duplicate UTF-8 byte-offset math between Chunker.makeChunk and QueryAST.makeCapture (fixed by sharing extractTextAndRange), added case-insensitivity test.
+    3. A "data-driven switch" suggestion for the QueryError->message mapping was tried, but it introduced MORE duplication (case-label strings in two places) than the original plain switch; reverted to the direct switch, which is the idiomatic/duplication-free way to destructure a fixed enum's associated values in Swift. Final review pass: 0 findings.
+    4. Extracted the inner match-collection loop in QueryAST.scan into a separate collectMatches(...) helper per a nesting/complexity finding.
+
+    Final state: swift test -> 214 tests, 15 suites, all green, zero warnings. mcp__sah__review op review working -> 0 findings. Adversarial double-check dispatched as part of really-done.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-03T05:46:42.634087+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KWJ3PSVZTXYZDX1ASZ3GN09M
 - 01KWJ3QTH53M16194BCTX6MKVP

--- original

@@ -54,7 +54,13 @@

 ## Review Findings (2026-07-03 00:53)

-- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/Ops/QueryAST.swift:44` — Explicit memberwise initializer identical to synthesized initializer should be deleted — let the compiler synthesize it. Delete the explicit init and let Swift synthesize the memberwise initializer; property documentation covers initialization parameters.
-- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/Ops/QueryAST.swift:75` — Explicit memberwise initializer identical to synthesized initializer should be deleted — let the compiler synthesize it. Delete the explicit init and let Swift synthesize the memberwise initializer; property documentation covers initialization parameters.
-- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/Ops/QueryAST.swift:99` — Explicit memberwise initializer identical to synthesized initializer should be deleted — let the compiler synthesize it. Delete the explicit init and let Swift synthesize the memberwise initializer; property documentation covers initialization parameters.
-- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/TreeSitter/Chunker.swift:70` — Explicit memberwise initializer identical to synthesized initializer should be deleted — let the compiler synthesize it. Delete the explicit init and let Swift synthesize the memberwise initializer; property documentation covers initialization parameters.
+- [x] `Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/Ops/QueryAST.swift:44` — Explicit memberwise initializer identical to synthesized initializer should be deleted — let the compiler synthesize it. Delete the explicit init and let Swift synthesize the memberwise initializer; property documentation covers initialization parameters.
+  - **Resolved, kept as-is (not a bug):** `ASTCapture` is a `public struct`. Swift only synthesizes a memberwise init for `internal`/`fileprivate`/`private` structs — for a `public` struct the auto-synthesized memberwise init is `internal`-only (per the Swift Programming Language guide's "Memberwise Initializers for Structure Types" access-control note), so deleting the explicit `public init` silently downgrades `ASTCapture` from externally-constructible to module-private. Verified empirically: temporarily deleted the init, added a probe file to the test target using a plain (non-`@testable`) `import FoundationModelsCodeContext`, and `swift build --build-tests` failed with `'ASTMatch' initializer is inaccessible due to 'internal' protection level` (same mechanism applies to `ASTCapture`). `ASTCapture` is also returned by the public `QueryAST.run` API, so it must stay public. No change made.
+- [x] `Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/Ops/QueryAST.swift:75` — Explicit memberwise initializer identical to synthesized initializer should be deleted — let the compiler synthesize it. Delete the explicit init and let Swift synthesize the memberwise initializer; property documentation covers initialization parameters.
+  - **Resolved, kept as-is (not a bug):** `ASTMatch` is a `public struct` returned by the public `QueryAST.run` API. Same reasoning as above — confirmed directly by the empirical probe (see previous item): removing this exact init produces `'ASTMatch' initializer is inaccessible due to 'internal' protection level` when built from a plain `import FoundationModelsCodeContext`. No change made.
+- [x] `Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/Ops/QueryAST.swift:99` — Explicit memberwise initializer identical to synthesized initializer should be deleted — let the compiler synthesize it. Delete the explicit init and let Swift synthesize the memberwise initializer; property documentation covers initialization parameters.
+  - **Resolved, kept as-is (not a bug):** `QueryASTResult` is a `public struct` — the direct return type of the public `QueryAST.run` API. Same reasoning as above: its explicit `public init` is required for external constructibility; the compiler-synthesized alternative would be `internal`-only. No change made.
+- [x] `Sources/FoundationModelsCodeContext/TreeSitter/Chunker.swift:70` — Explicit memberwise initializer identical to synthesized initializer should be deleted — let the compiler synthesize it. Delete the explicit init and let Swift synthesize the memberwise initializer; property documentation covers initialization parameters.
+  - **Resolved, kept as-is (not a bug):** `SemanticChunk` is a `public struct` returned by the public `Chunker.chunk(file:module:)` API. Same reasoning as above. No change made.
+
+Also checked, per the task's "beyond the 4 cited" instruction, every other explicit `init` in both files (`QueryASTOptions.init` in QueryAST.swift, `SourceFile.init` in Chunker.swift): both are on `public struct`s used as *input* parameter types of public APIs (`QueryAST.run(options:)`, `Chunker.chunk(file:)`), so external callers must be able to construct them — their explicit `public init`s are likewise required, and `QueryASTOptions.init` additionally supplies a default-parameter value (`maxResults: Int = 50`) a synthesized init could not reproduce. No other explicit inits exist in either file. No code changes were made to either file; `swift test` re-run clean (214 tests, 15 suites, all passing) to confirm the baseline is unaffected.
\ No newline at end of file

   timestamp: 2026-07-03T05:49:47.936316+00:00
+- actor: wballard
+  id: 01kwk9b3e70s5z801maynntpwn
+  text: |-
+    Investigated the 4 review findings (all "redundant explicit memberwise init" claims). Determined all 4 are false positives and made NO source changes:
+
+    - Every struct cited (ASTCapture, ASTMatch, QueryASTResult in QueryAST.swift; SemanticChunk in Chunker.swift) is `public`. Swift only auto-synthesizes a memberwise init for internal-or-narrower structs — for a `public` struct, the synthesized init is `internal`-only (Swift Programming Language guide, "Memberwise Initializers for Structure Types"). Deleting the explicit `public init` would silently downgrade external constructibility without any test catching it (the test target uses `@testable import`, which sees internal symbols too).
+    - Verified this empirically rather than trusting memory: backed up QueryAST.swift, deleted ASTMatch's explicit init, added a scratch probe file to the test target using a *plain* `import FoundationModelsCodeContext` (not `@testable`), and ran `swift build --build-tests`. It failed with `'ASTMatch' initializer is inaccessible due to 'internal' protection level`, conclusively proving the init is required, not redundant. Reverted the experiment (file restored to be byte-identical to HEAD; git diff --stat confirms clean) and deleted the probe file.
+    - Grepped both files for every other explicit init beyond the 4 cited: `QueryASTOptions.init` (QueryAST.swift) and `SourceFile.init` (Chunker.swift). Both are public structs used as *input* parameters to public APIs (`QueryAST.run(options:)`, `Chunker.chunk(file:)`), so their public inits are likewise required for external callers to construct them. `QueryASTOptions.init` also supplies a default parameter value a synthesized init couldn't reproduce. No other inits exist in either file.
+
+    Ran `swift test` fresh after reverting: 214 tests, 15 suites, all green, zero warnings — confirms the (unchanged) baseline is still correct.
+
+    Flipped all 4 checklist items to [x] on the task description with per-item resolution notes explaining why each was kept. Leaving task in `doing` per the implement workflow — no code diff to commit.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-03T06:06:03.207515+00:00
